name: bikar-medallion-10
convergenceNote: CONVERGED at the interim girih-era bar per docs/decisions/2026-05-28-medallion10-girih-ceiling.md (status ACCEPTED). Bar: A4 coverage >= ~85% AND A2 fold-balance @ fold-10 (CV <= ~5%) AND A6 >= 6/6 vs the girih-vocabulary baseline. iter-33 meets it: A4 86.13%, A2 CV 2.02%, A6 6/6, dominant_fold=10 @ conf 0.76. A5 band-integrity (the white strapwork weave) is NOT a gate for the girih class until Slice 4 strapwork overlay lands (bikar#653) — A5=0 is expected and accepted. Closes sacred-patterns#80 and #85.
remainingVisualGap: The render is a gap-free filled tile mosaic; the reference's white interlacing strapwork bands and crisp discrete star tiles are not yet rendered. This is the deferred Slice-4 strapwork overlay (bikar#653), which renders the SAME decoration lines as woven bands (render-style, not new geometry) and makes A5 the live metric.

convergence.structural: partial — wrong faces from decagon frame
91 faces, white bands, satellites via hankin (too short)
layer isolation failed — rays can't form faces alone
540 faces from full-chord satellites, too dense, engine fix: connect every on
91 faces, hankin on all 10 ring-1 circles, satellites merge into connected web
BREAKTHROUGH: 11 distinct rosettes! layers + connect every 4 + 0.3x radius
11 rosettes with center on C0 (too large), all connect every 4
11 same-size rosettes (center C1 + 10 satellites), interstitial gap remains
added Cmid radius 65 connect every 3 — A6 net +3, pixel +0.1, A2 cv slightly worse
Cmid moved to 80 + Cmid2 radius 50 — A6 net -3 regression, pixel -1.9; STAGNATION (A6 1/18 x 3 iters); session paused for approach pivot
chord-overlay / hankin / satellite refinements; best chord-overlay was iter-30 (composite 3/5)
best chord-overlay: ~11 teal/blue rosette clusters, 10-fold, composite 3/5; but NO white strapwork bands, NO crisp star tiles (girih-era high-water mark for the line-drawing philosophy)
single central girih decagon tile — proved girih VOCABULARY (encoder detected rosette+decagon types) but A6 0/18 (single tile has no full-radius extent), composite 2/5

manual 10-bowtie ring around the decagon — DETACHED SPOKES with white gaps, A4 halved to 48.6%, lost rosette type, composite 1/5. Hit the construction-philosophy CEILING stop-rule (3rd A6=0). Triggered present-options -> girih-ceiling decision doc.

CONVERGED (interim girih-era bar). Engine path Option C-double-prime: new `girih field decagonal 62 shells 2` DSL statement (growGirihField shell-BFS via attachGirihTile seam-solver) grows a gap-free 10-fold decagonal field. A1 PASS 100%, A2 85 (CV 2%), A4 86.13%, A6 6/6 vs girih baseline, dominant_fold=10 @ conf 0.76. A5=0 deferred (Slice-4 strapwork, bikar#653). Artifact source of record: qiyas calibration/i1-corpus/renders/medallion10-iter33/.

Wave-1 center-star fit (two parts): (a) connect every 4 ({10/4}, thin) -> hankin angle 54 (full fatness, slivers closed); (b) rotation fix — `divide C0 into 10 offset 18` phase-aligns hankin's edge-midpoint star (18deg native phase) to the reference's division-phase points. NEW ENGINE PRIMITIVE: DSL `divide <c> into N offset <deg>` exposes kernel divideCircle startAngle (Tenet 26 — was kernel-only). Census-equal (675, zero (type,sides) delta); fill+face_class identical. Gate: coverage 0.897->0.932, iou 0.707->0.751 vs the original {10/4}; eyeball points-on-gold-outline. Regression: packages/core/tests/dsl/divide-offset.test.ts (4 tests). Full bikar suite 964+3xfail green.

engineImprovements: girih field decagonal <edge> [shells N] — gap-free decagonal field generator (growGirihField + chooseTileForExposedEdge, Slice 1 commit cdc25c4; DSL wiring Slice 2 commit ddb2e98) (iter 33, Option C-double-prime)
openFollowUps: bikar#653 — Slice 4 strapwork overlay (bowtie/hexagon pocket-filling + band weaving): renders the field's decoration lines as woven white bands, recovering the reference's defining strapwork feature and making A5 the live metric. The remaining visual gap.
bikar#654 — DONE (A6 re-baselined to girih vocabulary, 0/10 -> 6/6).
bikar#655 — DONE (interim A4+A2 acceptance bar adopted).

wave-1 {10/3} refinement (owner 2026-06-15, task #30 follow-on) — owner approved wave 1 as ONE central star (structure gate set) but noted 'its 10/3': the central star should be a {10/3} star polygon, not the current hankin-derived build (iter-70 used connect every 4 = {10/4} -> hankin angle 54; session top-level starNotation is the stale {10/4}). Re-author wave-1's central star as {10/3} (denser/sharper points), re-render, eyeball vs reference, regression-test. Owner-gated build (bikar core); does NOT reopen the structure gate — it's a within-approved-grouping refinement.

stage_gates._doc: Stage-gated loop (structure->color->weave). Owner agreement on the stage's gate visual is recorded here; v1 records chat agreement. Schema: sacred-patterns iterate-construction-hypothesis SKILL.md -> 'The stage ladder'.

structure.approved_authority: Owner pick via wave1-regate-picker (wave1-regate-picker/index.html), 2026-06-15. PICK A: 'One shape — the lone central star'. Owner note verbatim: 'Its one shape, but its 10/3'. This visual-picker pick carries owner authority per the present-options-visually skill contract. The structure stage is APPROVED: wave 1 = a single central star (one authored shape, not split into disk+frame). Construction refinement (the star should be {10/3}, not the current hankin {10/4}-derived build) is captured as a follow-on task, NOT a gate blocker — the owner approved the grouping.

verdicts.note: Owner: 'obvious the structures do not match'. New protocol: match center-out radially; owner picks the shapes to match first by clicking them on the reference (review/structure-priorities.html -> review/structure-priorities.json). Detected medallion center (386, 361), diameter 738px, owner-validated via the annotator's Step 1.

Owner pick via wave1-regate-picker: PICK A — wave 1 = one central star (single authored shape, grouping confirmed; NOT split into disk + star-frame). Owner note verbatim: 'Its one shape, but its 10/3'. Structure gate APPROVED. The 10/3 construction-density refinement (current build is hankin-derived, owner wants a {10/3} star polygon) is a follow-on refinement task, recorded below in convergence + as a tracked task, not a structure blocker.

structure.protocol: wave-based center-out: exhaustive wave plan (input/reference-analysis/wave-plan/) gated by owner BEFORE construction; iterate wave-by-wave with per-wave diffs, never one-shot whole-image diff

wave_plan.waves: 22 same-KIND waves ordered inside-out (one wave = one kind of shape: midpoints equidistant from center, rotated copies, similar area, similar color). Counts are 10-fold (1, 10s, 20s, 40s) confirming the grouping.
wave_plan.flowers: 3 composite flowers (motifs): the middle flower (x1, 21 shapes = w1-w3), the inner flowers (x10 @ r~0.46, 15 shapes each = w4-w13), the outer flowers (x10 @ r~0.76, 21 shapes each = w14-w22) — every instance of a family holds the same wave-multiset (1 deviation = the known lost sliver in w21). Middle and outer both 21 shapes: the owner's 'similar to our middle shape' confirmed by count. A flower = build once, rotate 10 (DSL-native unit).
wave_plan.open_adjudications: wave 13 (turquoise ring r~0.59): seats 'inner' by distance, owner's eye said 'outer'; wave 15 (small navy stars r~0.65): seats 'outer', owner's list skipped it. The owner can now fix both directly in the studio (click a shape in the wave -> move its whole wave to the right flower -> Apply).
wave_plan.studio: tools/wave-plan-server.py <session-dir> --center 386 361 --diameter 738 — serves the plan on localhost; owner clicks any shape, fixes its wave/flower, Apply persists to wave-plan-overrides.json and re-plans

16.note: gate-bookkeeping stamp: wave-16 overlay (pattern.bkr L612-619) emits 40 deep_navy outer-fin faces, census-equal to the 40 reference non-fragment deep_navy shapes (r_frac 0.64-0.71). Already built since iter-57; iter-71 is frozen best. No .bkr edit (authoring would duplicate the overlay).

17.note: structure stamp: wave-17 overlay (pattern.bkr L629-632, 10 mirror-pairs / rotate 10) emits 20 six-sided faces @ r_frac 0.728-0.731, census-equal to the 20 reference non-fragment navy shapes (r_frac 0.723-0.733). STRUCTURE built since iter-58. COLOR CAVEAT: these 20 faces render deep_navy #3C3F47 (charcoal) via the layer-2 .deep_navy_wave class, but the reference shows them medium-navy ~#21356A. That tonal gap is a color-stage residual (owner-gated via portal, not loop-tunable per Tenet 7) — NOT a structure blocker. Same gate standard as wave 16. No .bkr edit.

18.note: structure stamp (invocation-gap, build-stamp NOT stage-gate approval): wave-18 overlay (pattern.bkr L633-641, layer 4, one rotate-10 cycle) emits 10 twenty-edge royal star-medallion faces at identical radius r_norm 1.000, evenly placed at 36deg around the outer ring — census-equal to the 10 reference royal star-medallions. CORRECTS the prior '2/10, 8 MISSING' survey, which was a premise error from normalizing the radial band against a different radius (Tenet C1/C2): all 10 emit. Class channel: render.svg data-face-class=royal_wave (30 total = 10x 20-edge wave-18 medallions + 20x 6-edge companions); gt.json has source_primitives, not face_class. Eyeball: 10 markers land on royal-blue star faces, 10-fold symmetric, matching reference placement (/tmp/w18-mark.png). Wave 18's hold was the radial-white strapwork defect (bikar task #19, suppress_radial engine fix), now SHIPPED + verified — outer band reads tangential, no radial spokes. Owner does final portal eyeball; stage gate NOT self-approved (stage_gates.structure.approved still null).

19.note: Wave 19 (deep_navy, outer edge, ref 20 real shapes @ renorm r 0.81-0.82): INVOCATION-GAP (stamp-only), NOT a geometry gap. iter-71 render emits 20 deep_navy_wave faces @ renorm r 0.834, paired around the 10 medallions (alt 25.1/10.9 deg gaps = 10 pairs), census-equal to the 20 reference deep_navy shapes. Prior survey 'w19 10/20, half missing' was a C1/C2 premise error (misread the band — there are TWO adjacent navy rings at r 0.834 and 0.864; the survey counted only one). Eyeball-confirmed: 20 markers land on the dark-slate #353941 navy star-faces in the outer band (NOT royal #2661BF, NOT white lattice, NOT empty). Proof: iterations/71/wave19-check.png + wave19-zoom-top.png. Owner portal eyeball still pending (owner_verdict null; do NOT self-approve the stage gate).

20.note: Wave 20 (navy outer-lobe pentagons, ref 20 navy #283B6E @ renorm r 0.879-0.889): INVOCATION-GAP (stamp-only). iter-71 emits 20 layer-1 navy #132A61 faces @ renorm r 0.846-0.851 on the dart axes (paired, even 10-fold ring). Ref #283B6E maps to palette navy #132A61 (RGB dist 30, vs royal 89.5 — same family). Eyeball-confirmed: 20 magenta markers on navy dart-lobes (iterations/71/wave2021-check.png). Census-equal.

21.note: Wave 21 (deep_navy small tiles, ref 39 real #404249 @ renorm r 0.870-0.915): INVOCATION-GAP (stamp-only). iter-71 emits 40 layer-2 deep_navy #3C3F47 faces @ renorm r 0.834-0.864 (two families x mirror pair x 10). Ref #404249 maps to palette deep_navy #3C3F47 (RGB dist 5.4 — near-identical). 40 vs ref-39 is fragment-count noise (ref drops 4 fragments from 43; construction emits full 40). Eyeball-confirmed: 40 green markers on dark-navy slivers (wave2021-check.png). Census-equal within fragment noise.

22.note: Wave 22 (FINAL — navy outer-rim triplets, ref 30 navy #25396C @ renorm r 0.961-1.000): INVOCATION-GAP (stamp-only). iter-71 emits 30 layer-1 navy #132A61 faces @ renorm r 0.917-0.936 as 10 triplets, one per star-tip. Ref #25396C maps to palette navy #132A61 (RGB dist 25.9, vs royal 92.1 — same family). The trailing `layer 0` at pattern.bkr L694 is the post-wave RESET, not the wave-22 tiles' layer — the connects (L689-693) are under `layer 1` (L680) = navy. Prior-tick 'rim renders ROYAL' was a radial-census misattribution (layer-0 base-field royal faces fill the GAPS around the navy overlay tiles; the overlay tiles themselves are navy). Eyeball-confirmed: 30 markers = 10 triplets on navy rim tips (iterations/71/wave22-check.png). Census-equal.

liveFront: 2026-06-15 — STRUCTURE GATE APPROVED by owner (wave1-regate-picker PICK A, 'one central star'; note 'Its one shape, but its 10/3'). stage_gates.structure.approved=true @ iter 71. Task #30 closed. Follow-on (NOT a blocker): re-author wave-1 central star as {10/3} per owner note (openFollowUps). Next stage = COLOR (stage_gates.color.palette_agreed still false — owner-gated). Also still owner-gated: PUSH bikar 50897af (#53 engine, unblocks /simplify step 4 + GHA cost) — batch with sacred-patterns f1ae423 (/simplify portal, committed this session). PRIOR liveFront (2026-06-14, verified vs iter-71 tree by LAYER census, Tenet C1/C2): STRUCTURE 22/22 WAVES STAMPED — all built + census-equal + eyeballed. Waves 16/18/19/20/21/22 were ALL INVOCATION-GAPS (faces emit correctly, just un-stamped); every 'missing'/'wrong-color' survey this session was a radial-census misattribution premise error (the trap bit 3 times — 18, 19, 20-22). The fix was to census by data-layer (each wave's overlay layer encodes its color: 1=navy 2=deep_navy 3=cobalt 4=royal 5=periwinkle 6=cyan_2; 0=base field), NOT by radial band (which mixes overlay tiles with base-field gap-fill at the outer edge). New durable memory: feedback_census_by_layer_not_radius.md; full retraction in ISSUES-OBSERVED.md 'Outer-edge waves 20-22 — ALL invocation-gaps'. Eyeball proofs: wave18/19/22-check.png + wave2021-check.png. RADIAL-WHITE FIX (task #19, suppress_radial) SHIPPED + verified earlier this loop (bikar 5-file engine extension + regression + docs, NOT committed). ALL OWNER-GATED NOW: stage_gates.structure.approved is NULL and ALL 22 waves have owner_verdict null — STRUCTURE STAGE IS READY FOR OWNER PORTAL EYEBALL + APPROVAL. Do NOT self-approve the stage gate. Next loop action depends on owner: if owner approves structure, advance to COLOR stage; otherwise address owner denials (fix lowest denied wave). 2 bikar commits HELD (3ff260a, deef89f) + this loop's uncommitted suppress_radial fix + sacred-patterns changes — do NOT commit/push without owner go-ahead (Tenet 22). Top-level status='converged-interim'/iter=33 STALE; 22-wave plan authoritative.
